Ukraine Conflict News





12 December 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • China firmly opposes illegal unilateral sanctions: Chinese FM on US' seizure of oil tanker off Venezuela, imposition of sanctions Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- China firmly opposes illegal unilateral sanctions and long-arm jurisdiction that lack a basis in international law and are not authorized by the UN Security Council, and opposes the abuse of sanctions, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday.
  • US slaps fresh sanctions on Venezuela amid escalated tensions Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- The United States has announced new sanctions on Venezuela, mounting more pressure on the energy-rich Latin American nation.
  • 'Act of state piracy': Iran blasts illegal US seizure of Venezuelan oil tanker Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- Iran has condemned the US Navy's attack on a commercial vessel carrying Venezuelan crude oil off the coast of the South American country as an "act of state piracy," warning about the consequences of such coercive measures.
  • U.S. intends to keep oil from seized tanker, eying more vessels off Venezuela People's Daily 12 Dec 2025 -- White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Thursday that the United States intends to keep the oil from the tanker the U.S. military seized Wednesday near the coast of oil-rich Venezuela.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Hegseth, Senior Leaders Attend Spacecom Relocation Ceremony DoD News 12 Dec 2025 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth joined numerous members of Congress and a handful of senior military officials today to mark the official relocation of U.S. Space Command from Peterson Space Force Base, Colorado, to Redstone Arsenal, Alabama.
  • New capabilities on horizon for African Lion 2026 Army News 12 Dec 2025 -- A multinational team of planners from military, government and industry sectors convened with the Royal Moroccan Armed Forces (FAR) in Agadir, Morocco, from Dec. 8-12, 2025, for the mid planning event (MPE) for African Lion 2026 (AL26).
  • Illinois National Guard unit plays vital role in U.S. military operations in Somalia Army News 12 Dec 2025 -- Approximately 155 Soldiers from the Illinois Army National Guard's Pontiac-based Alpha Troop, 2nd Squadron, 106th Cavalry Regiment protected U.S. personnel and equipment in the Horn of Africa from October 2024 to July 2025, improving security posture in a strategically important part of the world.
  • Year in Review: Transforming at the pace of change Army News 12 Dec 2025 -- The United States Army has consistently transformed to meet the needs of its people, equipment, tactics and strategy over the past 250 years. The year 2025 was no different. Army senior leaders demonstrated this year that no challenge is too great to overcome if new ways of thinking are applied to find solutions. The Army addressed quality-of-life concerns integral to Soldier morale, identified "out-of-the-box" methods to meet evolving requirements, and implemented advanced systems to gain a competitive edge over our adversaries.
  • Year in Review: Warfighting is the Army's top priority Army News 12 Dec 2025 -- United States Army Soldiers proved the most lethal and combat-ready force in the world in 2025. From showcasing advanced warfighting capabilities and tactics, securing and defending the nation from within our borders, to proving our skills in international conflict, there is no doubt the U.S. Army's training prepares Soldiers to fight and win.
  • Year in Review: Army meets recruiting goal, improves Soldier experience Army News 12 Dec 2025 -- As 2025 draws to a close, the United States Army reaffirmed its commitment to Soldiers after another incredible year. Throughout the year the Army spent 2025 preparing for a bigger and better 2026, elevating the Soldier experience and awarded performance for life-saving and historic actions.
  • CNO Navy 250th Commemoration Independence Hall Speech US Navy 12 Dec 2025 -- Speech by Adm. Daryl Caudle, Presented on 13 October 2025
  • Kuwaiti-led Exercise Enhances Combined Counter-Drone Capabilities CENTCOM 12 Dec 2025 -- U.S. forces completed a high-end, advanced counter-drone exercise in Kuwait with international partners, Dec. 12.
  • Coast Guard Cutter James conducts counter-drug patrol in Eastern Pacific Ocean US Coast Guard 12 Dec 2025 -- The crew of the U.S. Coast Guard Cutter James (WMSL 754) returned to their home port in Charleston, Wednesday, following a 92-day deployment conducting counter-narcotics operations in the Eastern Pacific Ocean in support of Operation Pacific Viper.
  • Coast Guard Cutter Harriet Lane returns home following 81-day patrol in Oceania US Coast Guard 12 Dec 2025 -- The crew of USCGC Harriet Lane (WMEC 903) returned to Honolulu Saturday following an 81-day patrol in support of Coast Guard Oceania District's Operation Blue Pacific.
  • NAVAIR Releases V-22 Comprehensive Review Findings US Navy 12 Dec 2025 -- Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) released the findings of a comprehensive review Dec. 12, 2025, on the V-22 Osprey aircraft flown by the Air Force, Marine Corps and Navy.
  • CMSSF highlights evolving warfighter demands, space threats at Spacepower 2025 United States Space Force 12 Dec 2025 -- The character of warfare is evolving, and the Space Force is adapting to meet the challenges of this new frontier, according to Chief Master Sgt. of the Space Force John Bentivegna. Speaking at the Space Force Association's Spacepower Conference in Orlando, Fla., on Dec. 11, Bentivegna emphasized the critical yet often unseen role of space in modern conflict and the qualities demanded of today's Guardians.
  • Space Force reinforces combat culture, talent development United States Space Force 12 Dec 2025 -- uardians and senior leaders gathered for a full day of connection prior to the Space Force Association's Spacepower Conference 2025, where Space Force officials emphasized warfighting culture, readiness and professional development as the service continues to refine its identity and expectations.
  • Marines Execute a Tactical Air Control Party Exercise USMC News 12 Dec 2025 -- Reserve Marine units from across the country assembled at Camp Shelby, Mississippi, for a Tactical Air Control Party exercise focused on joint terminal attack controller (JTAC) and joint fires observer (JFO) training and qualification sustainment. The event enhanced joint fires proficiency while allowing Marines to complete their annual certification requirements and refine their air support coordination skills.

  • F-35 cross-servicing demonstration highlights NATO cohesion NATO Allied Air Command 12 Dec 2025 -- Allied Air Chiefs witnessed a major milestone in European Airpower cooperation on 5 December 2025, as Royal Netherlands Air and Space Force weapons specialists conducted a cross-servicing weapons load on a U.S. Air Force F-35A Lightning II from the 48th Fighter Wing at RAF Lakenheath, during the European Air Chiefs Group meeting.
  • NATO Deputy Secretary General visits the Netherlands NATO 12 Dec 2025 -- On 11 and 12 December 2025, NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska travelled to the Netherlands for a series of meetings and engagements in Rotterdam, The Hague and Delft.

  • Putin Decree Forces Foreigners Seeking Russian Residency To Sign Army Contracts RFE/RL 12 Dec 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin has issued a little-publicized decree last month that requires many foreign men who seek permanent residency or citizenship to sign a contract with the military in the midst of Moscow's war against Ukraine, an RFE/RL investigation has found.

  • Hungary's Szijjarto Accuses NATO Chief of Undermining Ukraine Peace Talks Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte is inflaming tensions and undermining peace talks on Ukraine, and Hungary, as a NATO member, rejects his statements about the need to prepare for a war with Russia, Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto said on Friday.
  • "Horoshok": ammunition capable of destroying enemy FPVs and Mavic-class drones even with standard-issue small arms Ukraine MOD 12 Dec 2025
  • MoD procured over 600,000 adaptive clothing items under the 'Wounded Warrior Package' initiative Ukraine MOD 12 Dec 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal: The fastest way to strengthen Europe's security is to support Ukraine Ukraine MOD 12 Dec 2025
  • Denys Shmyhal: We have built an integrated anti-corruption management system Ukraine MOD 12 Dec 2025

  • Regular Press Briefing of the Ministry of National Defense on December 5, 2025 PRC MND 12 Dec 2025
  • 2025 Yearender: From design institute of carrier-based aircraft to V-Day military parade, GT witnesses China's equipment development in 2025 Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- The year of 2025 marks an unforgettable year for the development of China's weaponry and equipment, with many new additions to the arsenal of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) dazzling military enthusiasts. The Global Times reporters were fortunate to be granted the opportunity to witness some of the debuting events, recording the memorable moments first-hand.

  • Motions seeking Lai defense briefing sent to 2nd reading, negotiations CNA 12 Dec 2025 -- Opposition motions requiring President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) to take questions from the Legislature on his proposed NT$1.25 trillion special defense budget were sent directly to a second reading and cross-party negotiations on Friday.
  • U.S. urged to expedite F-16 collision avoidance system installation CNA 12 Dec 2025 -- Taiwan has asked the United States to expedite work on installing an automatic ground collision avoidance system (AGCAS) across its F-16V (Block 20) fleet, the Air Force said Thursday.
  • Defense chief defends awarding explosive import bid to furniture company CNA 12 Dec 2025 -- Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) on Thursday defended his ministry's decision to award a contract to import military-grade explosives to a company better known for selling furniture, after a lawmaker questioned the public procurement process.
  • Taiwan, Poland sign drone industry MOU to build non-China supply chain CNA 12 Dec 2025 -- A Taiwanese government-backed drone alliance and a Polish unmanned system industry group have signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) to develop a "non-China" supply chain for drones and work together on key technologies.

  • Ghost Bat hits the mark mid-air Australia DOD 12 Dec 2025 -- The Australian designed and manufactured MQ-28A Ghost Bat achieved another major milestone, becoming one of the first collaborative combat aircraft (CCA) to successfully engage an aerial target with a live weapon.
  • Investing in the Future of Australian Air Mission Training Australia DOD 12 Dec 2025 -- The Albanese Government is investing more than $300 million to transform air mission training, ensuring our Defence personnel are ready for the challenges of tomorrow.

  • HMNZS Aotearoa welcomed home after eight-month deployment New Zealand Defence Force 12 Dec 2025 -- Friends, family, haka, senior defence leaders and the Royal New Zealand Navy band were all on show on the wharves at Devonport Naval Base today to welcome home HMNZS Aotearoa and its 80-strong ship's company who have been deployed at sea since late March.

  • Conflict zone training success for P-8A Poseidon crews New Zealand Defence Force 12 Dec 2025 -- Royal New Zealand Air Force P-8A Poseidon crew have been testing the aircraft's self-protection capability in spectacular fashion above Manawatū skies recently.

  • UK launches new Military Intelligence Services as hostile threats surge UK MOD 12 Dec 2025 -- New unified organisation to speed up how Defence gathers and shares intelligence.

  • Japan-U.S. Defense Ministerial Telephone Conference (Summary) Ministry of Defense of Japan 12 Dec 2025

  • Aatmanirbharta means indigenous design & development of equipment, possessing the upgradation ability, and maintaining full control: Secretary (DP) India PIB 12 Dec 2025 -- Secretary (Defence Production) Shri Sanjeev Kumar has stressed on the need to achieve self-reliance in defence manufacturing to deal with conventional and unconventional challenges of today

  • The 8th edition of Pakistan Navy's Maritime Security Workshop concluded at Pakistan Navy War College, Lahore. ISPR Pakistan 12 Dec 2025

  • The 205th ROK-U.S. Joint Committee of the Status of Forces Agreement Joint Press Release ROK MFA 12 Dec 2025

  • Starting from 2027 the duration of conscript service will be 12 months Estonia MoD 12 Dec 2025 -- Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur sent a bill for approval, pursuant to which draftees will be called up for conscript service twice a year for a period of 12 months starting from 2027. The aim of this change is to ensure more permanent combat readiness for the Estonian Defence Forces with the introduction of new high-tech capabilities.

  • Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen: Finland will procure state-of-the-art air-to-air missiles for F-35 fighters Finnish MoD 12 Dec 2025 -- Finland's Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen has authorised the Finnish Defence Forces to procure AIM-120D-3 Advanced Medium Range Air-to-Air Missiles (AMRAAM) from the United States.
  • Finnish Defence Forces to place large orders for defence system services with Finnish companies Finnish MoD 12 Dec 2025 -- Minister of Defence Antti Häkkänen has authorised the Finnish Defence Forces to procure support services for military aviation, maritime defence, intelligence, surveillance and command and control from the Finnish companies Patria Aviation Oy and Insta Group Oy. The main part of deliveries will take place in 2026-2030.

  • PCG welcomes first female paratroopers Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) on Friday marked a new milestone after three women completed the PCG's airborne course and became the agency's first female paratroopers.
  • DND: Locally designed weapon system to boost PH's self-reliant defense Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. said the Controller Operated Battle-Ready Armament (COBRA) automated gun mount, developed by the Department of Science and Technology (DOST) and its partners, will greatly enhance the country's Self-Reliant Defense Posture (SRDP) Program.
  • PAF's modern assets play key role in 9th joint maritime drill with US Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine Air Force (PAF) said Friday its modern and major assets played a key role in the 9th bilateral maritime cooperative activity (MCA) between the Philippines and the United States, which took place in the West Philippine Sea (WPS) on Dec. 9-10.
  • PH, France conclude 2nd round of talks on visiting forces pact Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- The Philippines and France successfully concluded the two-day second round of negotiations on the proposed Philippines-France Status of Visiting Forces Agreement (SOVFA) on Thursday.

  • Joint Statement from The Combined Space Operations Initiative Principals' Board Meeting in Toulouse Government of Norway 12 Dec 2025 -- From December 2-4, 2025, the annual Combined Space Operations (CSpO) Initiative Principals' Board convened in Toulouse, France.

  • Ranking Member Shaheen, Colleagues Introduce Legislation to Restore and Safeguard U.S. Support for UNFPA US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 12 Dec 2025 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, Tim Kaine (D-VA), Peter Welch (D-VT), John Hickenlooper (D-CO), Jacky Rosen (D-NV), Patty Murray (D-WA), Chris Van Hollen (D-MD), Tammy Duckworth (D-IL), Cory Booker (D-NJ), Kirsten Gillibrand (D-NY), Catherine Cortez-Masto (D-NV), Bernie Sanders (I-VT), Jeff Merkley (D-OR), Dick Durbin (D-IL), Adam Schiff (D-CA) and Chris Coons (D-DE) introduced the Support UNFPA Funding Act, legislation to authorize U.S. contributions to the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) and reassert American leadership in supporting access to family planning globally, preventing maternal deaths and responding to gender-based violence.

  • Sweden and United States extend outer space cooperation Government Offices of Sweden 12 Dec 2025 -- Minister for Defence Pål Jonson has signed an extension of the existing framework agreement with the United States on cooperation in the research and use of outer space for peaceful purposes.

  • US launches 'Pax Silica' alliance to secure AI supply chains, counter China; move distorts market principles, bound to fail: analysts Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- The US is forming a coalition at a summit on Friday to counter China's dominant control of critical minerals and growing role as a center of artificial intelligence (AI) and other tech sectors, according to media reports. The move follows signing of a preamble agreement on Thursday between the US and Japan on such a partnership.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts For December 4, 2025

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 12 December 2025 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 12 Dec 2025

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hamas slams Israeli decision to legalize 19 settlements in occupied West Bank Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has strongly condemned an Israeli plan to legalize at least 19 Israeli settlements in the occupied West Bank.

  • Interim Gaza Government Should Link Back to Palestinian Authority - PA Deputy Minister Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- A technocratic interim government for administering the Gaza Strip should be directly linked to the cabinet of the Palestinian Authority (PA), and the issue is being actively discussed among mediators involved in the Israeli-Palestinian settlement, Palestinian Deputy Foreign Minister Omar Awadallah told Sputnik.

  • FM: Israel preparing for large-scale military operation against Lebanon Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- Lebanon's Foreign Minister Youssef Raji says his country has received warnings from Arab and international parties that the Israeli regime is preparing for "a large-scale military operation" against Lebanon.

  • Deep concerns for Palestinians amid intense Israeli raids in occupied West Bank UN News 12 Dec 2025 -- The UN human rights office, OHCHR, has expressed alarm at reports of a so-called "settler road" being built in the occupied West Bank.
  • UN General Assembly adopts resolution demanding Israel end Gaza aid restrictions Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- The United Nations General Assembly has adopted a draft resolution urging Israel to provide unrestricted humanitarian access to the Gaza Strip.
  • Nearly 795,000 Displaced Palestinians at Flood Risk after Storm Byron Makes Landfall in Gaza IOM 12 Dec 2025 -- Storm Byron, a severe storm system that triggered flooding in Greece and Cyprus before reaching Gaza earlier this week, has now made landfall, bringing heavy rainfall that has already flooded multiple displacement sites and placed nearly 795,000* displaced Palestinians at heightened risk. Rainfall is expected to continue in the hours ahead, further straining conditions for families already living in unsafe shelters.

  • U.S. No Vote on Anti-Israel UNGA Resolution US Dept. of State 12 Dec 2025 -- "Today, the United Nations General Assembly yet again passed an unserious and biased resolution, demonstrating the continued prevalence of anti-Israel bias over substantive diplomacy at the organization."

  • Clear Support for UN Resolution on Emergency Aid to Palestine Government of Norway 12 Dec 2025 -- Today, 139 countries voted in favour of a UN resolution expressing full support for the advisory opinion of the International Court of Justice.
  • Joint Statement by the Foreign Ministers of Pakistan, Egypt, Indonesia, Jordan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Türkiye, and United Arab Emirates Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Dec 2025 -- The Foreign Ministers of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Arab Republic of Egypt, the Republic of Indonesia, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the State of Qatar, the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Republic of Türkiye, and the United Arab Emirates reaffirm the indispensable role of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA) in safeguarding the rights and well-being of Palestinian refugees. For decades, UNRWA has carried out a unique mandate entrusted to it by the international community, providing protection, education, health care, social services, and emergency assistance to millions of Palestinian refugees in its areas of operation, in accordance with UN General Assembly Resolution 302 (1949). The adoption of the UN General Assembly resolution to renew UNRWA's mandate for an additional three years, reflects international confidence in the vital role played by the agency and the continuity of its operations.
  • Saudi Arabia and Several Countries Affirm UNRWA's Vital Role in Protecting Palestinian Refugee Rights SPA 12 Dec 2025 -- The foreign ministers of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, the Arab Republic of Egypt, the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan, the United Arab Emirates, the Republic of Indonesia, the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, the Republic of Türkiye, and the State of Qatar reaffirm the indispensable role of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East (UNRWA) in safeguarding the rights and well-being of Palestinian refugees

    • Other Conflicts

  • Statement by the ASP Presidency concerning the receipt of the OIOS fact-finding report on alleged misconduct by the ICC Prosecutor ICC 12 Dec 2025 -- The Presidency of the Assembly of States Parties informs that the fact-finding investigative report by the Office of the Internal Oversight Services (OIOS) on alleged misconduct by the ICC Prosecutor has been received by the President of the Assembly of States Parties on 11 December 2025 and has been forwarded immediately to the Panel of judicial experts established by the Bureau of the Assembly.
  • ICC With Dutch Support Preparing Measures in Case of New US Sanctions Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- The International Criminal Court (ICC), with the support of the Dutch government, is developing measures in case of new US sanctions, Dutch Foreign Minister David van Weel said on Friday.
  • Venezuelan Parliament Votes to Repeal Law Endorsing ICC's Rome Statute Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- The Venezuelan parliament said it voted to repeal the law endorsing the Rome Statute of the International Criminal Court (ICC).

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Ukraine UNOCHA 12 Dec 2025

  • Chinese military urges Philippines to stop provocation in South China Sea PRC MND 12 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese military on Friday urged the Philippines to immediately stop provoking incidents in the South China Sea.
  • Chinese PLA, CCG expel intruding Philippine aircraft, vessels Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Southern Theater Command and the China Coast Guard (CCG) issued separate statements on Friday, announcing that they had they had taken necessary measures to expel Philippine small aircraft and vessels that recently illegally intruded into the airspace of China's Huangyan Dao and the waters off Xianbin Jiao, respectively.

  • Tehran to host regional meeting on developments in Afghanistan: Foreign Ministry spokesperson IRNA 12 Dec 2025 -- The spokesperson for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs has announced plans to hold a regional meeting in Tehran for dialogue and exchange of views regarding developments related to Afghanistan.
  • Tehran to host regional meeting on developments in Afghanistan Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- The Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman has announced that Tehran will host a regional meeting to discuss the latest developments in Afghanistan.

  • UN mission's exit marks milestone in Iraq's post-conflict transition UN News 12 Dec 2025 -- After more than two decades working alongside Iraq through war, political upheaval and the fight against ISIL terrorists, the UN Assistance Mission there (UNAMI) ends its mandate on 31 December - a closure its top official describes as "honourable, dignified, and earned".

  • Israel admits it inflicted less damage to Iran than initially thought Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- Israel has admitted that it inflicted less damage to Iran during the 12-day war in June than it initially thought, a report says.

  • Separatist Group Jaish Al-Adl Rebrands As Wider Iranian Opposition RFE/RL 12 Dec 2025 -- The Sunni militant group Jaish al-Adl says it has joined forces with several smaller Baluch paramilitary groups to form a new coalition targeting Iran's clerical establishment.

  • UK leadership imperative as Myanmar's crisis deepens ahead of junta's announced sham elections: UN expert OHCHR 12 Dec 2025 -- A UN expert today urged the United Kingdom to help mobilise international opposition to the Myanmar military junta's claim that the election that it will stage later this month is legitimate, while taking other measures to strengthen its support of human rights in Myanmar.

  • Ethiopia/Eritrea: Statement by the Spokesperson on the anniversary of the Algiers Agreement European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 Dec 2025 -- Twenty-five years ago today, Ethiopia and Eritrea, with the support of the international community, concluded the Algiers Agreement to establish peace and demarcate a common border. It was followed by the historic 2018 peace agreement, which reaffirmed both countries' commitment to respect the border as established by the Algiers Agreement and its subsequent Boundary Commission.

  • Peace falters as fighting in eastern DR Congo raises fears of regional war UN News 12 Dec 2025 -- Civilians across eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) are facing collapsing health services and mass displacement as fighting spreads, undermining recent peace efforts and raising fears of a wider regional crisis.
  • Eastern Democratic Republic of Congo Crisis Risks Spillover, Senior UN Official Warns Security Council, Calling for Swift Action on Resolution 2773 (2025) UN Security Council 12 Dec 2025 -- The situation in the Democratic Republic of the Congo remains highly unstable, with continuing violence and a deepening humanitarian emergency that is putting civilians at grave risk and raising wider regional concerns, a senior UN official warned the Security Council today.
  • Press release on the escalation of the situation in the east of the Democratic Republic of the Congo Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Dec 2025
  • We condemn the recent M23 offensive and takeover of Uvira in eastern DRC: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 12 Dec 2025 -- Statement by Jennifer MacNaughtan, UK Minister Counsellor, at the Security Council meeting on the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Sudan UN Secretary-General 12 Dec 2025
  • Sudan war: Aid teams say deal struck to reach stricken El Fasher UN News 12 Dec 2025 -- In Sudan, deep concerns persist for the many tens of thousands of people believed to still be trapped in El Fasher in the Darfur region, but UN aid agencies believe they may soon get access to the embattled city.
  • WFP warns of more displacement in Sudan as fighting rages in Darfurs and Kordofans amidst shrinking resources WFP 12 Dec 2025 -- This is a summary of what was said by WFP Director of Emergency Preparedness and Response, Ross Smith, to whom quoted text may be attributed - at a press briefing In Geneva today. Mr Smith participated in the briefing remotely from Rome.
  • UK sanctions commanders responsible for Sudan atrocities and pledges additional humanitarian support as Cooper says atrocities must not go unpunished UK FCDO 12 Dec 2025 -- The UK has sanctioned individuals suspected of atrocities including mass killings, sexual violence, and deliberate attacks on civilians in El Fasher, Sudan.

  • Joint Statement on Haiti Force Generation Conference US Dept. of State 12 Dec 2025 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America and Canada on behalf of the Standing Group of Partners on December 12.
  • Security Council Speakers Call for Rigorous Sanctions Compliance to Address Multidimensional Crisis in Haiti UN Security Council 12 Dec 2025 -- As the Security Council was updated today on the work of its subsidiary body devoted to the Haiti sanctions regime, speakers stressed that these measures must be strictly implemented if they are to be effective in addressing the country's many crises.

  • Unanimously Adopting Resolution 2806 (2025), Security Council Extends Al-Shabaab Sanctions Regime, Panel of Experts UN Security Council 12 Dec 2025 -- The Security Council today extended until 30 November 2026 its sanctions on weapons entering Somalia and its authorization for Member States to intercept vessels transporting them, while simultaneously renewing until 31 December 2026 the mandate of the Panel of Experts assisting the Al-Shabaab sanctions regime.
  • This resolution mandates a strong package of measures to degrade Al-Shabaab: UK Explanation of Vote at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 12 Dec 2025 -- Explanation of Vote by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Somalia.

News Reports

  • Meeting of the International Forum Peace and Trust: Unity of Goals for a Sustainable Future President of Russia 12 Dec 2025 -- In Ashgabat, Vladimir Putin participated in the forum marking the International Year of Peace and Trust, the International Day of Neutrality, and the 30th anniversary of Turkmenistan's permanent neutrality.
  • Meeting with President of Turkiye Recep Tayyip Erdogan President of Russia 12 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin met with President of Turkiye Recep Tayyip Erdogan on the sidelines of the Peace and Trust: Unity of Goals for a Sustainable Future International Forum.
  • Meeting with President of Turkmenistan Serdar Berdimuhamedov President of Russia 12 Dec 2025 -- Following the plenary session of the International Forum Peace and Trust: Unity of Goals for a Sustainable Future, Vladimir Putin met with President of Turkmenistan Serdar Berdimuhamedov.
  • Press release on BRICS countries' sherpa/sous-sherpa meeting Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 12 Dec 2025
  • Council decides to prohibit transfers of immobilised Central Bank of Russia assets back to Russia EC/CEU 12 Dec 2025 -- The Council today decided to prohibit, on a temporary basis, any transfers of Central Bank of Russia assets immobilised in the EU back to Russia. This decision was taken as a matter of urgency to limit damage to the Union's economy.
  • Tsahkna: EU decision confirms that Russia will pay for its actions Estonia MFA 12 Dec 2025 -- The Council of the European Union decided today that €210 billion worth of assets belonging to the Central Bank of Russia will remain frozen until Russia ends its war of aggression against Ukraine, compensates Ukraine for the damage caused, and its military activities no longer pose a threat to the EU's economy.
  • Bank of Russia Launches Battle Against Euroclear for Illegal Asset Actions Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- The Bank of Russia said on Friday that it had filed a statement of claim with the Moscow Arbitration Court against the Euroclear depository for recovery of losses caused to the regulator.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on December 12, 2025 PRC MofA 12 Dec 2025
  • China issues new policy paper on Latin America, Caribbean after 9 years Press TV 12 Dec 2025 -- China has released its third policy paper on Latin America and the Caribbean (LAC), serving as a comprehensive guide for enhancing China-LAC relations.
  • Safeguarding natl sovereignty, security, devt interests is highest principle of 'One Country, Two Systems': FM on Jimmy Lai's case Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- Responding to a foreign media reporter's question about the Jimmy Lai case, the Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said on Friday that safeguarding national sovereignty, security, and development interests is the highest principle of "One Country, Two Systems."
  • China to work with ASEAN to align development strategies, resist unilateral bullying: Chinese FM Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- China regards ASEAN as its closest cooperation partner and is ready to continue providing mutual support and trust with ASEAN, align development strategies, create cooperation highlights, uphold open regionalism, pursue true multilateralism, resist any form of unilateral bullying and Cold War mentality, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi said here on Thursday.
  • All parties must abide by market economy principles, FM spokesperson addresses on US-led rare-earth supply chain pact Global Times 12 Dec 2025 -- "We have noted relevant reports. All parties should adhere to the principles of a market economy and fair competition and work together to maintain the stability of the global supply chain," Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun stated at a regular press conference on Friday.
  • PhilSA: Chinese rocket debris possibly fell near Palawan Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- The Philippine Space Agency (PhilSA) said the debris from a Chinese rocket launched on Friday possibly fell 23 nautical miles (NM) away from Puerto Princesa and 21 NM away from Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park.

  • Reunification the only direction for Taiwan's future: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 12 Dec 2025 -- Reunification is the only direction for Taiwan's future, a Chinese mainland spokesperson said on Thursday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 12 Dec 2025

  • Press Release on Enlarged Meeting of 13th Plenary Meeting of Eighth Central Committee of WPK KCNA 12 Dec 2025 -- The Enlarged Meeting of the 13th Plenary Meeting of the Eighth Central Committee of the Workers' Party of Korea was held from Dec. 9 to 11 to sum up the major Party and state affairs in 2025 and to decide on a series of important issues at present including the preparations for the Ninth Congress of the WPK.

  • Iranian Noble Prize Laureate Narges Mohammadi Arrested RFE/RL 12 Dec 2025 -- Iran's 2023 Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has been arrested, along with several other human rights activists during a memorial ceremony in the northeastern city of Mashhad.
  • Total revision of the Ordinance on Measures against the Islamic Republic of Iran Federal Council - Swiss Government 12 Dec 2025 -- On 12 December, the Federal Council decided to adapt the sanctions against Iran to the status prior to the conclusion of the Vienna Agreement on Iran's nuclear programme. To make that possible, it conducted a total revision of the Ordinance on Measures against the Islamic Republic of Iran. The new ordinance enters into force at 11pm on 12 December.
  • Meeting with President of Iran Masoud Pezeshkian President of Russia 12 Dec 2025 -- On the sidelines of the forum, President Vladimir Putin met with President of the Islamic Republic of Iran Masoud Pezeshkian.
  • Putin: Russia supports Iran on nuclear issue IRNA 12 Dec 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin says that Tehran and Moscow maintain close and continuous contact on sensitive international matters, including Iran's nuclear issue, and emphasized that Russia supports Iran.
  • Iran to Continue Cooperation With Russia in Security Sphere Sputnik 12 Dec 2025 -- Russia and Iran are working on strengthening bilateral ties in the field of security, the chairman of the Iranian Parliament's National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, Ebrahim Azizi, said on Friday.

  • Meeting with President of Iraq Abdul Latif Rashid President of Russia 12 Dec 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held a meeting in Ashgabat with President of the Republic of Iraq Abdul Latif Rashid.

  • Transcript of Special Briefing by MEA on Prime Minister's visit to Jordan, Ethiopia and Oman (December 12, 2025) India MEA 12 Dec 2025

  • Pakistan: Imran Khan's solitary confinement and inhumane detention conditions must end, UN expert says OHCHR 12 Dec 2025 -- The UN Special Rapporteur on torture, Alice Jill Edwards, today urged the Government of Pakistan to take immediate and effective action to address reports of inhumane and undignified detention conditions of former Prime Minister Imran Khan, warning that they could amount to torture and other inhuman or degrading treatment.

  • PM meeting with Belgian Prime Minister Bart de Wever: 12 December 2025 Gov.UK 12 Dec 2025

  • Honduras: Statement by the Spokesperson on the electoral process European External Action Service (EEAS) 12 Dec 2025 -- The European Union reiterates its support for the current electoral process in Honduras and the work that the National Electoral Council is carrying out despite all the technical difficulties. The EU calls on all candidates to wait and respect the counting process.

  • Marcos seeks deeper ties with China, Chile Philippine News Agency 12 Dec 2025 -- President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. has expressed hope that the Philippines' bilateral relations with China and Chile will further improve under the leadership of the two countries' newly designated ambassadors.
